extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus attacked solo inside 1km from a four-rider breakaway on Côte de Chaon, winning by 7 seconds after breakaway survived multiple climbs.
tactical_patterns: GAUDIN Damien collected KOM points at every categorized climb to steal polka dots without winning stage from breakaway.
KWIATKOWSKI Michał took yellow by staying in chase group on final climb, avoiding sprinter gruppetto without bridging to break.
AG2R La Mondiale delayed chase until Côte de Chaon, allowing breakaway margin despite COSNEFROY Benoît attempting late bridge with MADOUAS.
CORT Magnus attacked natural sprinter NEILSON inside 1km when quartet dynamics discouraged cooperation, exploiting hesitation rather than pure power.

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: breakaway tactician correctly identified moment to attack solo when stronger climbers neutralized each other's sprint threat
rider_signals.GAUDIN Damien: positive: prioritized KOM jersey over stage win, systematically cresting climbs first to accumulate points without exhausting for victory
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: defending champion dropped on Côte de Saint-Michel, confirming continued poor form from early-season weakness
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: dropped on same climb as SOLER despite being previous year's third-place finisher and best young rider at Giro
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: positive: calculated effort to take yellow by finishing in chase group, avoiding energy expenditure of bridging or sprinter losses
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: attacked on final climb despite riding entire 2018 Tour with broken shoulder, showing recovered form for aggressive racing
rider_signals.HENAO Sergio: positive: 2017 Paris-Nice champion attacked final climb, demonstrating continued ability to animate GC stages despite not making winning move
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: struggled on Côte de Trèves despite only second categorized climb, surrendering yellow earlier than expected for pure sprinter
